response:
## What `src/billing.ts` does

A 310-line module that owns invoice + refund logic for plans (including the `"enterprise"` tier). Two anchor functions:

- **`calculateRefundPreview`** (lines 40‚Äì44) ‚Äî name suggests a pure calculation, but the body calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail`. The preview is actually a side-effecting operation.
- **`generateInvoice`** (lines 50‚Äì253) ‚Äî a 204-line "god function" that mixes responsibilities and reads wall-clock time at multiple points (lines 57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273).

A comment at line 39 encodes a policy that doesn't appear to live in code: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."* That rule is load-bearing but invisible to the type system.

## Files to also read before editing

- **`src/billing.test.ts`** ‚Äî the existing coverage is thin: one test has zero assertions ("renders the enterprise plan", lines 4‚Äì6) and another uses only weak matchers ("creates an invoice", lines 8‚Äì10). Don't trust a green run here.
- **`src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts`** ‚Äî the literal `"enterprise"` is scattered across all four files. Any change to enterprise behaviour likely needs to touch (or at least audit) these.
- **`src/date.ts`** ‚Äî already exists and also reads the clock directly; if you introduce a clock abstraction, this is the sibling to align with.
- **`docs/billing.md`** ‚Äî part of a tenant/account/organisation/team/workspace alias drift cluster; worth a glance to see which vocabulary the docs use.

## Risks the scan flagged on this file

| Charge | Severity | Why it matters for an edit |
|---|---|---|
| God Function (`generateInvoice`, 204 lines) | high | Editing one branch easily misses interactions in another |
| Temporal Recklessness (7√ó direct `Date` calls) | medium | Domain code is non-deterministic; tests can't pin time |
| False Identity (`calculateRefundPreview`) | medium | Callers may treat it as pure ‚Äî it isn't |
| God File (310 lines, 5 top-level fns) | medium | Hidden local coupling outside the agent's loaded window |
| Logic in the Alibi (line 39 comment) | medium | A real policy lives in prose, not code |
| Unfinished Business (4 TODO/FIXME, lines 183‚Äì308) | low | Hard to tell which logic is load-bearing |

Cross-cutting risks that touch billing.ts indirectly: the `"enterprise"` string-sprinkle (crime_00007) and the route-metadata drift between `/settings/billing` labels in `src/nav/registry.ts` ("Plans") vs `src/nav/sidebar.ts` ("Billing") vs the page title ("Subscription") ‚Äî relevant if your edit changes how the billing surface is named or navigated.
